binary_removal_strategy.core_principle: Remove DB binary blobs from pushed git history. Preserve all local files on disk. Replace binary evidence with: manifest JSON, SHA256 hash file, row count, schema summary, integrity check result.

preserve_local_db.action: DO NOT DELETE — must remain at full 94924-row migrated state

preserve_local_backup.action: DO NOT DELETE — must remain as P188 pre-migration evidence

manifest_to_create_in_p195.purpose: Replace binary evidence in git with a text manifest containing DB metadata, SHA256, row count, and schema summary

binary_removal_strategy.note_on_sha256_file: backups/p188_lottery_v2_backup_20260601_153821.db.sha256 STAYS in git — it is the lightweight audit trail

approach_1.description: git reset --soft HEAD~1 to undo P191 commit while keeping staged state. Unstage DB binaries. Add .gitignore and manifest. Recommit without binaries.
steps: 0. VERIFY: sqlite3 lottery_api/data/lottery_v2.db 'SELECT COUNT(*) ...' = 94924 (safety check before reset)
1. git reset --soft HEAD~1  (undoes P191 commit; keeps all 109 files staged; local DB files untouched)
4. Create docs/db_migration_manifest_p188_p191.json with DB metadata, sha256, row counts
5. Append gitignore entries for *.db paths to .gitignore
7. git commit -m 'P182-P191: reconcile replay governance (no DB binaries)' --no-edit  [with same body]
8. VERIFY: sqlite3 lottery_api/data/lottery_v2.db 'SELECT COUNT(*) ...' = 94924  (DB must still exist)
10. VERIFY: git show HEAD --name-only | grep '.db$' = empty  (no .db in new commit)
risks: reset --soft undoes HEAD~1 only — the 6 commits ahead structure changes to 5 non-binary + no binary commit
If dirty working tree has unexpected files, they may be accidentally staged after reset
Must NOT run git add -A after reset — only explicit files
P124-P128 commits are NOT affected (they don't contain DB binaries)
benefits: Clean: rewrites only the P191 commit
Local DB files preserved in working tree
Resulting commit is push-safe (no large binaries)
approach_1.prerequisite: Explicit P195 authorization phrase required before execution

approach_2.description: Remove binaries from index with git rm --cached, then amend the P191 commit.
steps: 1. git rm --cached lottery_api/data/lottery_v2.db  (removes from index, keeps local file)
5. git commit --amend  (rewrites P191 in place; keeping same message)
6. VERIFY local DB still exists and has 94924 rows
risks: git rm --cached does NOT delete local file, but operator must confirm
amend rewrites P191 commit hash — changes the commit identity
Must ensure git rm --cached is used, NOT git rm (which would delete the file)
approach_2.recommendation: ACCEPTABLE ALTERNATIVE — slightly higher confusion risk than Approach 1

approach_3.description: Create new feature branch from origin/main. Cherry-pick P191 changes excluding DB binaries. Push feature branch. Open PR.
steps: 7. git commit -m 'P182-P191: reconcile replay governance (no DB binaries)'
9. Open PR to main; wait for replay-default-validation CI to pass
risks: Requires branch creation and checkout — must be explicitly authorized
P124-P128 commits on local main are NOT pushed via this path (they stay local)
Local main diverges from the feature branch
benefits: Cleanest PR workflow — respects branch protection from the start
Does not rewrite local main commit history
Feature branch can be tested by CI before merge
approach_3.recommendation: PREFERRED FOR PR WORKFLOW — but adds branch complexity

approach_4.description: Do not rewrite anything. Keep P191 commit as-is on local main. Export a text manifest of DB state. Pause remote reconciliation.
risks: Remote repo (origin/main) remains at P124 state — no P125-P191 work published
Local-only state is fragile — if local disk fails, all work since P124 is lost
Does not resolve binary problem; just defers it
approach_4.recommendation: ACCEPTABLE as short-term hold only; not a resolution

rationale: git reset --soft HEAD~1 is clean and well-understood
It precisely undoes only the P191 commit while keeping all content staged
Unstaging the DB binaries is explicit and auditable
The local DB files are NOT affected by the reset (they stay at 94924 rows)
Results in a clean, push-safe commit without large binaries
After Approach 1, Approach 3 (feature branch PR) should be used to satisfy branch protection
cto_recommendation.combined_path: Approach 1 (remove binaries from local commit) → Approach 3 (create feature branch and PR) → CI passes → merge to main
critical_safety_rules: NEVER run git rm lottery_api/data/lottery_v2.db — only git rm --cached or git restore --staged
NEVER run git reset --hard — only git reset --soft
VERIFY DB rows = 94924 BEFORE and AFTER the reset
Do NOT push the current P191 commit as-is
Do NOT force push to main
cto_recommendation.gitignore_importance: Adding DB paths to .gitignore is critical to prevent future accidental commits of the 96MB production DB. Once done, the DB can grow freely without approaching GitHub's 100MB hard limit.
cto_recommendation.manifest_importance: The docs/db_migration_manifest_p188_p191.json replaces binary evidence with verifiable metadata: sha256, row counts, byte sizes. Any auditor can verify the migration by running local DB queries against the sha256 in the manifest.
cto_recommendation.ci_note: The replay-default-validation CI check runs on PRs. After binary removal, the commit will be small enough for CI to handle. Ensure the CI check passes before merging.
